name: Universal ID Validator
description: Real-time, check-character-aware validation of participant and specimen IDs on REDCap data-entry forms and surveys. Validates single-ID fields and pooled fields (several IDs in one box), catches typos as they are typed, and can optionally block saving on an invalid ID. Uses the same verified ISO/IEC 7064 engine as the QR/ID generator; no JavaScript Injector required.

authors.name: CHPR
authors.institution: Bamenda Center for Health Promotion and Research (CHPR)

project-settings.name:
Universal ID Validator
Add one rule per group of fields. Each rule picks the check-character method the IDs were minted with, which fields it applies to, and what happens on an invalid ID. Leave a rule's advanced boxes blank to use the defaults.
How to log invalid IDs caught on the server (API / import / JavaScript-off)

choices.name: Store a SHA-256 hash of the value (default — correlates repeats without storing the ID)
Log the event but not the value
Store the raw value (only if your data-governance policy allows identifiers in module logs)
Do not log server-side detections at all
Single ID (one participant ID per field)
Pooled (several IDs jammed into one field)

sub_settings.name: Check-character method (must match how the IDs were minted)

choices.name: ISO 7064 Mod 11,2 — digits, check 0-9 or X

sub_settings.name: Optional format pattern (regex for ONE ID). Required if method is 'No check character'. JavaScript regex; avoid nested quantifiers like (a+)+.
Separators to ignore before checking (default: - / space _ | \ )
Pooled only: extra characters to keep when splitting a pooled field (rarely needed)
Pooled only: exact ID length(s), comma-separated (e.g. 10 or 10, 12). Overrides the min/max range below.
Pooled only: minimum ID length (default 8). Used only if exact lengths are blank.
Pooled only: maximum ID length (default 14; must be less than 2× the minimum).
Pooled only: expected number of IDs in the field (optional; warns if different). Positive whole number.
On an invalid ID (client-side; the server always logs — see above)

choices.name: Advisory — warn and ask before saving
Compulsory — block the browser save until fixed
